description: The BNSS replaces the Code of Criminal Procedure (CrPC) 1973. It governs the procedure for investigation and trial of criminal cases in India.
key_changes_from_crpc: Zero FIR concept — FIR can be registered at any police station
Timeline for investigation — charge sheet within 90 days
Electronic communication for summons and warrants
Provisions for attachment of property in terrorism cases
Community service as sentence for minor offences

important_sections.37: Arrest without warrant — when police may arrest
important_sections.41: Arrest safeguards — rights of arrested person
important_sections.47: Rights of arrested person — information of grounds
important_sections.173: Police report — charge sheet / closure report
important_sections.258: Power to stop proceedings and discharge accused
important_sections.439: Special powers of High Court / Session Court regarding bail
